summary: Implemented backend soft-delete for documents. In document-upload.ts: added AND deleted_at IS NULL to getDocumentUploadById, getDocumentUploadsByPetId (both branches), and getDocumentGroup; changed deleteDocumentUpload from DELETE to UPDATE setting deleted_at; added new softDeleteDocumentWithCascade function that soft-deletes the upload, optionally soft-deletes linked extraction items (cascade=true), and always nulls created_record_id/created_record_type on linked items to unlink health records. In document-extraction.ts: added deleted_at IS NULL to getDocumentExtractionByUploadId, getDocumentExtractionById, getDocumentExtractionItems, and getDocumentExtractionItemById. In document-import.ts: imported softDeleteDocumentWithCascade, removed storage deletion from DELETE endpoint, replaced hard-delete with soft-delete logic (cascade param required for processed docs, simple soft-delete for unprocessed), and added deleted_at IS NULL filters to the inline extraction item counts query. In pets.ts: added deleted_at IS NULL filters to the source-document lookup JOIN query.
notes_for_reviewer: The softDeleteDocumentWithCascade always nulls created_record_id/created_record_type on extraction items regardless of cascade flag. This is correct per spec: both cascade=true and cascade=false remove the source links from health records; cascade=true additionally soft-deletes the extraction items themselves.

The DELETE endpoint now requires the cascade query param when the document status is 'completed' or 'pending_review'. Unprocessed documents (uploaded, failed, processing, pending) do a simple soft-delete with no cascade requirement.
Storage files are intentionally not deleted -- soft delete preserves everything in storage.

document-extraction.ts was not listed in the plan's file list but required deleted_at IS NULL filters on its read queries to be consistent. All three read functions (getDocumentExtractionByUploadId, getDocumentExtractionById, getDocumentExtractionItems, getDocumentExtractionItemById) were updated.
pets.ts source-lookup query was found via grep and updated to exclude soft-deleted documents and items.
TypeScript compilation is clean (tsc --noEmit, no output).
